_description: Step-by-step process for onboarding a new brand into the APXlab system. Input: website URL. Output: complete brand vault ready for ad production.

steps.action: Scrape the main website URL provided
Identify ALL websites/domains for this brand
look_for: B2B vs DTC sites (may have separate domains)
steps.note: The DTC-facing site is the one we care about for ads. B2B site may have better product detail though

steps.action: Scrape the DTC store/shop page specifically
what_to_extract: Any bundles, combos, or subscription options

steps.action: Search for competitor and market context
what_to_extract: How is this brand positioned vs competitors
What do customers love/hate in reviews
What ad angles are competitors using

scoring_criteria.AOV: Higher price = more ad budget per sale = more room for CPA
scoring_criteria.value_story: Does this product have a clear 'why this vs alternatives' angle?
scoring_criteria.visual_appeal: Is the product photogenic? Does it look good in someone's hand?
scoring_criteria.emotional_hook: Does this product solve a painful problem or deliver a desirable outcome?
scoring_criteria.repeatability: Is this a one-time purchase or recurring? (recurring = higher LTV = more ad budget)
scoring_criteria.bundle_opportunity: Can we upsell from single to bundle/combo?

steps.action: Select STAR PRODUCT for initial ad campaign
rules: Start with ONE product. Don't split focus across multiple products on day 1
Pick the product with highest AOV that still has a strong emotional hook
If a bundle/combo exists that saves money vs buying separately, lead with that (value angle built in)
The star product should have the broadest audience appeal within the target demo

steps.action: Create product JSON for EACH product
template_fields: packaging (type, material, dimensions, colors, label_elements, scale_in_hand)

sources_priority: 3. Download from Amazon/retail listings if available
4. Screenshot from website as last resort
images_needed: Product front/hero shot (REQUIRED for image gen reference)
Product packaging/unboxing (useful for unboxing hook ads)
steps.future: AUTO-DOWNLOAD: When image scraping is available, automatically download all product images from store pages and save to products folder

steps.minimum: 1 primary persona for the star product
template_fields: pain_points (what problems drive them to this product)
objections (why she might NOT buy, and the counter)
ad_character_match (should look like / should NOT look like)

checklist: brand.json exists and has all fields
Star product .json exists with pricing, packaging, ad_angles
At least 1 product reference image saved
Campaign folder created with empty subfolders
All pricing matches DTC store (not third-party sites)

missing_items: anything we still need from client
output_format.recommended_next_step: Generate 10 ad concepts for [star product] targeting [persona]

phase_5_fast_track.name: Optional: Immediate Concept Generation
phase_5_fast_track.description: If user wants to move fast, skip approval and go straight to concepts

steps.action: Generate 10 ad concepts using concept-generator framework

steps.output: 10 concepts with confidence ratings, top 3 recommended

image_auto_download.plan: When running in Claude Code or n8n: automatically scrape all tags from product pages, filter for product shots (not icons/decorative), download to products folder, name appropriately
image_auto_download.tech: Puppeteer or Playwright for JS, or requests + BeautifulSoup for Python

competitor_ad_scraping.plan: Pull competitor ads from Meta Ad Library to see what angles and hooks they're using. Feed into concept generator for differentiation
competitor_ad_scraping.tech: Meta Ad Library API or scraper

social_proof_aggregation.plan: Pull Google reviews, Trustpilot, Amazon reviews automatically. Extract themes and real quotes for testimonial angles
social_proof_aggregation.tech: Review scraping APIs or manual extraction
